Binary effect-head

Four-class report Overview Scores Pairs Distribution Profiles Mutations Files
ReCLIP + binary XGBoost effect head · directed 5x5 scan

In-the-wild PPI binary mutation-effect report

Binary effect head outputs only two probabilities: no effect (0) and effect (1). This rerun also includes self-pairs, so all 5 target proteins are paired with all 5 partners.

In-the-wild means these are candidate protein sequences scanned outside a curated labeled benchmark; the report prioritizes model signals rather than claiming experimentally validated interactions.

25 directed target-partner pairs self-pairs included 2 x A100 parallel inference
input protein sequences
directed target-partner pairs, including self
total inference rows, WT + mutant
mutant delta rows

Score Definitions

The binary XGBoost head outputs proba_0 no effect and proba_1 effect.

delta_effect = mutant_proba_1 - WT_proba_1

Positive delta means the mutation increases predicted effect probability relative to the WT baseline at the same target position and partner. Negative delta means the mutation makes the site look more like no effect.

Interpretation Boundary

This is still a mutation-effect classifier, not a standalone binary PPI interaction classifier. Pair rankings are useful for prioritizing model signal, but should be interpreted together with target direction, self-pair context, sequence profiles, and top mutation tables.

Protein Pair Priority

Top protein pairs by top20 mean delta_effect

A->B and B->A are combined for cross-protein pairs; self-pairs use their single A->A direction. The mean of top site-level deltas is more stable than a single maximum.

Pair highlights

Directed pair heatmap

Top directed pairs

Delta Distribution

Overall delta_effect distribution

Thresholds are empirical for this run. q95 marks strong positive delta_effect signals; q99 marks the extreme positive tail. Negative values are expected and mean effect probability decreased relative to WT.

Top mutations by positive delta_effect

Protein sequence delta profile by partner

21 aa

The x-axis is target sequence position. The y-axis is the maximum binary delta_effect among reachable mutations at that site for the selected target -> partner pair. Partner includes self-pair options in this rerun.

Result Files

The HTML embeds compact summary data for visualization. The linked TSV files provide exact rows for filtering, reanalysis, and traceability.
